items.title: Andes-virus cluster — MV Hondius cruise (South Atlantic), multi-country
items.summary: 12 confirmed and 1 probable/inconclusive Andes-virus cases on the MV Hondius, departed Ushuaia, Argentina on 2026-04-01 with stops in Antarctica, South Georgia Island, Tristan da Cunha, Saint Helena, Ascension Island. CDC met the ship at Las Palmas, Canary Islands on 2026-05-07. 3 deaths reported. Andes virus is the only hantavirus with documented person-to-person transmission.
items.citation: WHO Disease Outbreak News (2026-DON611). (2026). Andes-virus cluster — MV Hondius cruise (South Atlantic), multi-country. Retrieved 2026-07-23 from https://www.who.int/emergencies/disease-outbreak-news/item/2026-DON611.

items.title: WHO DON — Hantavirus cluster, cruise ship (2026-DON604)
items.summary: As of 27 May, a total of 13 cases, including three deaths, have been reported (case fatality ratio 23%). Eleven cases have been laboratory-confirmed for Andes virus (ANDV) infection, and two are probable cases. Given the long incubation period of up to six weeks, it is not unexpected that cases continue to be reported until the end of the six weeks since las
